docs.title: LongProbe
docs.text: Sub-second RAG regression testing for production pipelines

docs.title: Overview
docs.text:
"Did my last commit break retrieval?" — now you know in seconds.

LongProbe is a sub-second RAG regression harness. Define your Golden Questions once, run longprobe check on every commit, and get an exact diff of which document chunks were lost in your latest change — before your users notice.

Think pytest --watch for your RAG pipeline.

docs.title: Why LongProbe?
docs.text:
Every RAG developer faces the same silent killer: you refactor chunking strategy, upgrade LangChain, or add a new document — and your retrieval silently degrades. DeepEval and RAGChecker are heavyweight evaluation frameworks meant for batch analysis, not fast regression checks in a dev loop.

LongProbe gives you instant feedback:

  • ⚡ Sub-second checks on small golden sets
  • 🔍 Exact diffs showing which chunks were lost/gained
  • 📊 Recall scores with per-question breakdown
  • 💾 Baseline tracking to catch regressions over time
  • 🧪 pytest integration for existing test suites
  • 🔌 Pluggable adapters for any vector store

docs.title: Quick Example
docs.text:
# Install
pip install longprobe

# Initialize
longprobe init

# Define your golden questions in goldens.yaml
# Configure your vector store in longprobe.yaml

# Run checks
longprobe check

# Save baseline
longprobe baseline save --label v1.0

# Compare after changes
longprobe diff --baseline v1.0

docs.title: Part of the Long Suite
docs.text:
LongProbe is part of the EnDevSols Long Suite of RAG tools:

  • LongParser - Document ingestion and chunking
  • LongTrainer - RAG chatbot framework
  • LongTracer - Hallucination detection
  • LongProbe - Retrieval regression testing ← You are here

Together they cover the full RAG pipeline from ingestion to production monitoring.

docs.title: Core Capabilities
docs.text:
• ⚡ Sub-second checks on small golden sets
  • 📋 Golden Questions + Required Chunks defined in simple YAML
  • 🔍 Three match modes: exact ID, text substring, semantic similarity
  • 📊 Recall Score with per-question breakdown
  • 🔄 Regression diff: exactly which chunks were lost/gained
  • 💾 SQLite baseline store: compare against any previous run

docs.title: Developer Experience
docs.text:
• 🧪 pytest plugin: integrate into existing test suites
  • 🖥️ Beautiful CLI with Rich tables, JSON, and GitHub Actions output
  • 👀 Watch mode: auto re-run on file changes
  • 🏗️ CI/CD ready: fails pipeline on regression

docs.title: Integrations
docs.text:
• 🔌 Pluggable adapters: LangChain, LlamaIndex, Chroma, Pinecone, Qdrant
  • 🌐 HTTP adapter: test any RAG API
  • 🐍 Python API: programmatic access to all features

docs.title: Changelog
docs.text:
All notable changes to this project will be documented in this file.

The format is based on Keep a Changelog, and this project adheres to Semantic Versioning.

docs.title: Added
docs.text:
• Professional MkDocs documentation site with Material theme
  • Three live demo GIFs showcasing key features:
  • Test RAG Retrieval (quick validation)
  • Monitor RAG Quality (detailed analysis)
  • Detect Regressions (baseline comparison)
  • Comprehensive documentation structure:
  • Getting Started guides (Installation, Quick Start, Configuration)
  • User Guide (Golden Questions, Match Modes, CLI Reference, Python API, Baseline Management)
  • Integration guides (Vector Stores, Pytest, CI/CD, LangChain, LlamaIndex)
  • Demo pages with detailed explanations
  • API Reference
  • Contributing guide
  • GitHub Actions workflow for automatic documentation deployment
  • Custom CSS for improved documentation UI
  • Professional README with logo, badges, and demo GIFs

docs.title: Changed
docs.text:
• Updated README with centered logo and GitHub raw URLs for images
  • Improved documentation navigation with collapsible sidebar sections
  • Enhanced logo visibility in documentation header

docs.title: Fixed
docs.text:
• Logo display issues in documentation
  • Asset paths for GitHub Pages compatibility

docs.title: Added
docs.text:
• Initial release of LongProbe
  • Golden question management with YAML configuration
  • Three match modes: ID, text, and semantic similarity
  • Baseline storage and regression detection using SQLite
  • CLI with multiple output formats (table, JSON, GitHub Actions)
  • Pytest plugin integration for test suite integration
  • Retriever adapters:
  • ChromaDB direct adapter
  • Pinecone direct adapter
  • Qdrant direct adapter
  • LangChain retriever wrapper
  • LlamaIndex retriever wrapper
  • HTTP/REST API adapter with configurable mapping
  • Question generation with LLM support (OpenAI, Anthropic, Gemini, Ollama)
  • Auto-capture functionality to build golden sets from live retrievers
  • Watch mode for continuous testing during development
  • GitHub Actions reusable workflow for CI/CD integration
  • Comprehensive test suite with 172 unit tests
  • Rich CLI output with color-coded tables and progress indicators
  • Diff reporting showing exactly which chunks were lost/gained
  • Per-question recall scoring with configurable thresholds
  • Support for question tags and metadata
  • Environment variable expansion in configuration files
  • Document parsing for multiple formats (TXT, MD, CSV, JSON, PDF, DOCX, PPTX, XLSX)

docs.title: Documentation
docs.text:
• Comprehensive README with quick start guide
  • CLI reference documentation
  • Python API examples
  • Configuration reference
  • Match mode explanations
  • GitHub Actions integration examples
  • Example configuration files and golden sets

docs.title: Contributing to LongProbe
docs.text: Thank you for your interest in contributing to LongProbe! This guide will help you get started.

docs.title: 1. Fork and Clone
docs.text:
# Fork the repository on GitHub, then clone your fork
git clone https://github.com/YOUR_USERNAME/LongProbe.git
cd LongProbe

docs.title: 2. Install Dependencies
docs.text:
# Install with development dependencies
uv sync --dev

# Or with pip
pip install -e ".[dev]"

docs.title: 3. Run Tests
docs.text:
# Run unit tests
uv run pytest tests/unit/ -v

# Run all tests including integration
uv run pytest tests/ -v --run-integration

# Run with coverage
uv run pytest --cov=longprobe --cov-report=html

docs.title: 4. Lint and Format
docs.text:
# Check code style
uv run ruff check src/

# Auto-fix issues
uv run ruff check --fix src/

# Format code
uv run ruff format src/

docs.title: 1. Create a Branch
docs.text:
git checkout -b feature/your-feature-name
# or
git checkout -b fix/your-bug-fix

docs.title: 2. Make Your Changes
docs.text:
• Write clear, concise code
  • Add tests for new features
  • Update documentation as needed
  • Follow existing code style

docs.title: 3. Test Your Changes
docs.text:
# Run tests
uv run pytest tests/

# Check linting
uv run ruff check src/

# Format code
uv run ruff format src/

docs.title: 4. Commit Your Changes
docs.text:
git add .
git commit -m "feat: add new feature"
# or
git commit -m "fix: resolve bug in X"

Use conventional commit messages: - feat: - New feature - fix: - Bug fix - docs: - Documentation changes - test: - Test changes - refactor: - Code refactoring - chore: - Maintenance tasks

docs.title: 5. Push and Create PR
docs.text:
git push origin feature/your-feature-name

Then create a Pull Request on GitHub.

docs.title: Code Style
docs.text:
We use Ruff for linting and formatting:

# Check style
uv run ruff check src/

# Format code
uv run ruff format src/

docs.title: Writing Tests
docs.text:
# tests/unit/test_feature.py
import pytest
from longprobe import LongProbe

def test_feature():
    """Test description."""
    # Arrange
    probe = LongProbe(...)

    # Act
    result = probe.some_method()

    # Assert
    assert result == expected

docs.title: Running Specific Tests
docs.text:
# Run specific test file
uv run pytest tests/unit/test_scorer.py -v

# Run specific test
uv run pytest tests/unit/test_scorer.py::test_recall_calculation -v

# Run with markers
uv run pytest -m "not slow" -v

docs.title: Building Docs Locally
docs.text:
# Install docs dependencies
pip install mkdocs-material mkdocstrings[python]

# Serve docs locally
mkdocs serve

# Build docs
mkdocs build

docs.title: Writing Documentation
docs.text:
• Use clear, simple language
  • Include code examples
  • Add links to related pages
  • Test all code examples

docs.title: Pull Request Process
docs.text:
1. Update Documentation - If you add features, update docs
  2. Add Tests - New features need tests
  3. Pass CI - All tests must pass
  4. Code Review - Address reviewer feedback
  5. Squash Commits - Clean up commit history if needed

docs.title: Release Process
docs.text:
(For maintainers)

  1. Update CHANGELOG.md
  2. Bump version in pyproject.toml
  3. Create git tag: git tag v0.2.0
  4. Push tag: git push origin v0.2.0
  5. GitHub Actions will build and create release

docs.title: Configuration
docs.text:
LongProbe uses two main configuration files:

  • goldens.yaml - Defines your golden question set
  • longprobe.yaml - Configures retriever, embedder, and scoring

docs.title: Complete Example
docs.text:
retriever:
  type: "chroma"
  chroma:
    persist_directory: "./chroma_db"
    collection: "my_documents"

embedder:
  provider: "local"
  model: "text-embedding-3-small"
  dimensions: 1536

scoring:
  recall_threshold: 0.8
  fail_on_regression: true

baseline:
  db_path: ".longprobe/baselines.db"
  auto_compare: true

docs.title: ChromaDB
docs.text:
retriever:
  type: "chroma"
  chroma:
    persist_directory: "./chroma_db"
    collection: "my_documents"

docs.title: Pinecone
docs.text:
retriever:
  type: "pinecone"
  pinecone:
    index_name: "my-index"
    api_key: "${PINECONE_API_KEY}"
    namespace: ""

docs.title: Qdrant
docs.text:
retriever:
  type: "qdrant"
  qdrant:
    collection: "my_collection"
    host: "localhost"
    port: 6333
    api_key: "${QDRANT_API_KEY}"

docs.title: HTTP API
docs.text:
retriever:
  type: "http"
  http:
    url: "http://localhost:8000/api/retrieve"
    method: "POST"
    headers:
      Authorization: "Bearer ${API_TOKEN}"
    body_template: '{"query": "{question}", "top_k": {top_k}}'
    response_mapping:
      results_path: "data.chunks"
      text_field: "content"
      id_field: "id"

docs.title: OpenAI
docs.text:
embedder:
  provider: "openai"
  model: "text-embedding-3-small"
  api_key: "${OPENAI_API_KEY}"
  dimensions: 1536

docs.title: Hugging Face
docs.text:
embedder:
  provider: "huggingface"
  model: "sentence-transformers/all-MiniLM-L6-v2"

docs.title: Local (No API)
docs.text:
embedder:
  provider: "local"
  model: "text-embedding-3-small"

docs.title: Scoring Configuration
docs.text:
scoring:
  recall_threshold: 0.8        # Minimum recall to pass
  fail_on_regression: true     # Exit 1 if regression detected

docs.title: Baseline Configuration
docs.text:
baseline:
  db_path: ".longprobe/baselines.db"  # SQLite database path
  auto_compare: true                   # Auto-compare vs latest baseline

docs.title: Environment Variables
docs.text:
Use ${VAR_NAME} syntax to reference environment variables:

retriever:
  type: "pinecone"
  pinecone:
    api_key: "${PINECONE_API_KEY}"

Set in your environment:

export PINECONE_API_KEY="your-key-here"

docs.title: Configuration Reference
docs.text: Section Field Type Default Description retriever type string "chroma" Adapter type retriever.chroma persist_directory string "" ChromaDB path retriever.chroma collection string "" Collection name retriever.pinecone index_name string "" Pinecone index retriever.pinecone api_key string "" API key retriever.qdrant host string "localhost" Qdrant host retriever.qdrant port int 6333 Qdrant port embedder provider string "openai" Embedding provider embedder model string "" Model name embedder dimensions int 0 Embedding dimensions scoring recall_threshold float 0.8 Min recall to pass scoring fail_on_regression bool true Exit 1 on regression baseline db_path string .longprobe/baselines.db SQLite path baseline auto_compare bool true Auto-compare vs baseline

docs.title: Requirements
docs.text:
• Python 3.9 or higher
  • pip or uv package manager

docs.title: Install with pip
docs.text: pip install longprobe

docs.title: Install with uv (Recommended)
docs.text:
uv is a fast Python package installer:

uv pip install longprobe

docs.title: Optional Dependencies
docs.text: LongProbe supports various vector stores and embedding providers through optional dependencies:

docs.title: ChromaDB Support
docs.text:
pip install longprobe[chroma]
# or
uv pip install longprobe[chroma]

docs.title: OpenAI Embeddings
docs.text: pip install longprobe[openai]

docs.title: Pinecone Support
docs.text: pip install longprobe[pinecone]

docs.title: Qdrant Support
docs.text: pip install longprobe[qdrant]

docs.title: All Optional Dependencies
docs.text: pip install longprobe[all]

docs.title: Specific Combinations
docs.text: pip install longprobe[chroma,openai]

docs.title: Development Installation
docs.text:
For contributing to LongProbe:

# Clone the repository
git clone https://github.com/ENDEVSOLS/LongProbe.git
cd LongProbe

# Install with development dependencies
uv sync --dev

# Or with pip
pip install -e ".[dev]"

docs.title: Verify Installation
docs.text:
longprobe --version

You should see output like:

LongProbe version 0.1.0

docs.title: Next Steps
docs.text:
• Quick Start Guide - Get started in 5 minutes
  • Configuration - Configure your vector store and embeddings

docs.title: Quick Start
docs.text: Get up and running with LongProbe in 5 minutes.

docs.title: 1. Install LongProbe
docs.text: pip install longprobe

docs.title: 2. Initialize Your Project
docs.text:
longprobe init

This creates three files:

  • .longprobe/ - Directory for baseline storage
  • goldens.yaml - Your golden question set
  • longprobe.yaml - Configuration file

docs.title: 3. Define Golden Questions
docs.text:
Edit goldens.yaml to define your test cases:

name: "my-rag-golden-set"
version: "1.0"

questions:
  - id: "q1"
    question: "What is the refund policy?"
    match_mode: "text"
    required_chunks:
      - "30-day money-back guarantee"
      - "full refund within 30 days"
    top_k: 5
    tags: ["policy", "critical"]

  - id: "q2"
    question: "How do I reset my password?"
    match_mode: "text"
    required_chunks:
      - "click forgot password"
      - "check your email"
    top_k: 5

docs.title: 4. Configure Your Vector Store
docs.text:
Edit longprobe.yaml:

retriever:
  type: "chroma"
  chroma:
    persist_directory: "./chroma_db"
    collection: "my_documents"

embedder:
  provider: "local"
  model: "text-embedding-3-small"

scoring:
  recall_threshold: 0.8
  fail_on_regression: true

docs.title: 6. Save a Baseline
docs.text: longprobe baseline save --label v1.0

docs.title: 7. Make Changes and Compare
docs.text:
After making changes to your RAG system:

longprobe diff --baseline v1.0

This shows you exactly what changed:

LongProbe Results
╭─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────── Summary ───────╮
│ Overall Recall:  0.95                                                     │
│ Pass Rate:       0.95  (19/20)                                            │
│ Regressions:     1 question(s) degraded                                   │
│ vs Baseline:     -0.05                                                    │
╰───────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────╯

Regressions detected:
  q5: Recall dropped from 100% to 50%
    Lost chunks: ["specific technical detail"]

docs.title: Development Loop
docs.text:
# Watch mode - auto re-run on changes
longprobe watch

docs.title: CI/CD Integration
docs.text:
# Fail pipeline on regression
longprobe check --output github

docs.title: Python API
docs.text:
from longprobe import LongProbe
from longprobe.adapters import create_adapter

adapter = create_adapter("chroma", collection_name="docs", persist_directory="./db")
probe = LongProbe(adapter=adapter, goldens_path="goldens.yaml")

report = probe.run()
assert report.overall_recall >= 0.85

docs.title: Golden Questions
docs.text: Golden questions are the foundation of LongProbe testing. They define what your RAG system should retrieve.

docs.title: What is a Golden Question?
docs.text:
A golden question consists of:

  • Question: The query to test
  • Required Chunks: Document chunks that must be retrieved
  • Match Mode: How to verify chunks (ID, text, or semantic)
  • Top-K: Number of results to retrieve
  • Tags: Optional metadata for organization

docs.title: Basic Example
docs.text:
questions:
  - id: "q1"
    question: "What is the refund policy?"
    match_mode: "text"
    required_chunks:
      - "30-day money-back guarantee"
      - "full refund within 30 days"
    top_k: 5
    tags: ["policy", "critical"]

docs.title: Match Modes
docs.text: See Match Modes for detailed explanations.

docs.title: Best Practices
docs.text:
1. Start Small - Begin with 5-10 critical questions
  2. Be Specific - Test specific facts, not general topics
  3. Use Tags - Organize by importance or category
  4. Test Edge Cases - Include difficult queries
  5. Update Regularly - Add questions when bugs are found

docs.title: Next Steps
docs.text:
• Match Modes - Understand matching strategies
  • CLI Reference - Run your tests

docs.title: Match Modes
docs.text: LongProbe supports three match modes for verifying retrieved chunks.

docs.title: ID Match
docs.text:
Exact string match on chunk/document IDs.

match_mode: "id"
required_chunks:
  - "doc_a_chunk_3"
  - "doc_b_chunk_7"

docs.title: Text Match
docs.text:
Case-insensitive substring matching.

match_mode: "text"
required_chunks:
  - "30-day money-back guarantee"

docs.title: Semantic Match
docs.text:
Word-frequency cosine similarity.

match_mode: "semantic"
semantic_threshold: 0.80
required_chunks:
  - "Only the CFO and CEO may authorize payments"

docs.title: Choosing a Match Mode
docs.text:
• ID: When you control chunk IDs
  • Text: For exact phrases
  • Semantic: When wording may vary
